pr.title: [Drafts] won't compile, contains individual decompiled functions
pr.author: Ka-lua

summary: Closed unmerged draft PR introducing a non-compiling src/drafts.c scratch file intended as a cherry-pickable collection of individually decompiled functions. The slice contains one simple Fighter-related function, func_800C88A0, linked to a decomp.me scratch and melee/ft/code_8008521C.s, which clears several Fighter fields.

key_files.role: New non-compiling draft file containing a single decompiled function candidate and decomp.me reference.

what_changed: Added src/drafts.c as a deliberately non-compiling holding file for individual decompiled functions.
Documented the intended workflow: cherry-pick functions from drafts.c when decompiling the module they belong to.
Added a decomp.me scratch link for the included function: https://decomp.me/scratch/VBrFf.
Added a note tying the function to melee/ft/code_8008521C.s.
Included melee/ft/fighter.h and defined void func_800C88A0(Fighter* f).
The function clears f->x2226_5, f->x2226_6, f->x2134, f->x2030, and f->x2226_8 to zero.
smart_moves: Kept the draft function isolated from normal build expectations by explicitly marking the file as not supposed to compile.
Preserved provenance with both a decomp.me scratch URL and the original assembly file path, making the snippet easier to verify or relocate later.
Used the existing Fighter type and field names rather than introducing speculative semantic names.
decomp_lessons: Scratch decompilation snippets are most useful when they include provenance: decomp.me link, target assembly path, and the exact function symbol.
For unmerged draft material, avoid treating the code as project-approved or build-integrated; it is evidence of a candidate match only.
When field semantics are unknown, preserving raw struct field names such as x2226_5 and x2030 is safer than inventing names.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained generated-style names such as func_800C88A0.
Unknown Fighter fields retained offset-style names such as x2226_5, x2226_6, x2134, x2030, and x2226_8.
The scratch file was named drafts.c to communicate that it was not part of the normal source layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Function candidate was associated with melee/ft/code_8008521C.s for later cherry-picking into the correct module.
A decomp.me scratch link was provided as matching evidence, with the PR body stating that functions match on decomp.me unless specified otherwise.
The implementation is a simple sequence of zero assignments to Fighter fields, likely intended to reproduce straightforward store-zero assembly.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that integrated func_800C88A0 into the fighter module.
Search for references to decomp.me scratch VBrFf in later history.
Check whether src/drafts.c or the drafts branch influenced later decomp scratch workflows.
